title: [CI:DOCS] ci: Automatically add "podman machine" label
body:
Because I want to track this sub-area myself.

BTW...any opinions on cleaning the labels up in this project?  I myself like the "area/XYZ" naming scheme.  It's used in Kubernetes ([example](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/122557)) and in bootc, etc.

If agreed I'm happy to make the switch here.

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
LGTM
@cevich @baude @edsantiago @mheon PTAL

Tentative LGTM. My only frustration is that the label has a space in it, and the [github labeler action](https://github.com/actions/labeler) does not explicitly document how to deal with that. It's reasonable to assume that doublequotes are the way, but who knows? I'm OK with merging this and immediately submitting a test PR to see if it works.

> My only frustration is that the label has a space in it,

Yes, I had a suggestion to change that in my original comment:

> BTW...any opinions on cleaning the labels up in this project? I myself like the "area/XYZ" naming scheme. It's used in Kubernetes (https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/122557) and in bootc, etc.

If no one objects it's trivial to rename "podman machine" :arrow_right: area/machine ...
whoops, I meant to comment on that. There are 200+ existing issues with the existing tag, so I'd be reluctant to change it. But not my call (I don't use the tag).

> There are 200+ existing issues with the existing tag, so I'd be reluctant to change it. But not my call (I don't use the tag).

Renaming a label name updates everywhere, instantly.   I believe in implementation the label name is a cosmetic identifier for a unique value.  In fact I'll Just Do It right now so we can see the effect...
Done: https://github.com/containers/podman/labels/area%2Fmachine
@ashley-cui @baude ptal
LGTM
OK now reworked under the assumption we'll stick with area/machine

/approve
/lgtm
/hold

Please do not merge this until @baude has given his blessing.

FWIW I've tried to grep the source tree and docs for instances of `podman machine` with the word "label", and found none. Likewise my email archives. I can't find any documentation or scripts that rely on the existing name. But still, let's allow @baude to have the final say.

/hold cancel

tyvm, contributions welcome!  @edsantiago thanks for your dillegence
/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude*, *cgwalters*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [baude,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Tested in #21151. Worked. Thanks everyone.

Tbh I find this name change totally inconsistent, we did not have any area/... labels so far. We have plenty of other labels that are some form of area (remote, network, compose, HTTP API, pasta, slirp4netns, etc...) so either we change all of them or none. I don't really care about what the name is (I have no issue with area if this is what people like) but it should be consistent across all labels.

Seems fair, yes.  As of right now, it looks like all those other labels are typically added manually; it's easy to change their names.  I have a (very slight) preference for area/ myself just because I think "namespacing" labels makes them easier to read.  I also like to have a consistent color for area/ labels as they're typically mutually exclusive.  But...dunno.  systemd by contrast doesn't namespace for the equivalent.

It really depends on what people like to do with labels, so far I (and some others) apply labels manually to issues in order to better sort/find them later on. I never really cared about labels on pull requests because I personally look at almost all of them anyway and I find it relatively easy to find an old PR even without any labels because I usually just look in the git log and search for the commit sha.

If others like you who are only interested in a specific area benifit from these labels then sure we can have this action to add them to PRs as well based on changed files. That however also adds a bit of noise from generic changes, i.e. https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21154 would likely get a bunch of area labels if you use this labeler action for more areas. And for some areas it is really not clearly separated in code so hard to apply automatically.

I don't think areas are mutually exclusive (at least not how we use these labels today), I can have bugs that are both in the network and kube area. Or for example pasta is a sub area of network, so if it has the pasta label it should likely always have the network label.

I was on PTO so my feedback is probably too late.  But for the record / in case it matters: I also dislike `alrea/` as it seems redundant.  I think just a simple `machine` label would be fine (assuming no other conflicts).
This is all easy to change, I only have a slight preference for area/ myself; since consensus seems to be against, I put up https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21203
